summary: Config-only verification for Play Store versionCode fix. Verified /app/frontend/app.json and /app/frontend/eas.json contents, then ran `npx expo config --type public` to confirm Expo's config resolver produces the same values that EAS Build will bake into the next .aab. All 8 assertions from the review request PASS. Backend + Expo supervisor services remain RUNNING and backend /api/ returns HTTP 200.
action_items: User can now trigger a fresh EAS Build (Emergent Publish -> Build Android). The generated .aab will carry versionCode=112 (> the rejected 111), so Google Play Console will accept the upload.

For every future release, manually bump both expo.android.versionCode AND expo.ios.buildNumber (keep them in sync) plus expo.version in app.json. Since appVersionSource is now 'local', EAS will no longer auto-increment; the value in app.json is the source of truth.
critical_code_review_comments: Note (informational, not a bug): `npx expo config` resolver output shows android.permissions has duplicated ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION and ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION plus an extra RECORD_AUDIO entry injected by plugins. This is cosmetic — Android merges duplicates at manifest merge time — but the main agent may want to deduplicate the explicit permissions list in app.json in a follow-up cleanup.
eas.json production build has no `autoIncrement` (correct, per the fix). Preview/development builds also have no autoIncrement, which is consistent with appVersionSource=local.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This iteration only verified static config files (app.json + eas.json) and the Expo config resolver output. No app code or backend endpoints were exercised beyond a single /api/ health probe. If the user later reports the new .aab still gets rejected, first re-check that the freshly built .aab actually carries versionCode=112 (using `aapt dump badging` on the downloaded artifact) — that is the definitive on-device proof, which we cannot run here without EAS cloud credentials.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: eas.json had cli.appVersionSource='remote', which delegates version tracking to EAS's server-side counter. That counter got stuck at 111 (likely because a prior build was uploaded to Play Store but the EAS counter didn't advance, or the counter was manually set). Since app.json had no explicit versionCode/buildNumber, every new build re-used 111 -> Play Store rejected duplicates. Fix: switched to appVersionSource='local' and pinned explicit versionCode=112 / buildNumber='112' / version='1.0.1' in app.json. Removed the now-inapplicable autoIncrement flag from the production build profile. Verified via `npx expo config --type public` that these values are what EAS Build will use.
